hypothesis: If a ReAct-based GPT-4o agent is prompt-tuned and given tool access to the physics-based Nb–O simulator, then it will achieve equal or higher success rates on the designed three-step synthesis planning challenges while visiting fewer states (i.e., greater computational efficiency) than an A* search with a 100-bin discretized state space, because the LLM can leverage high-level reasoning and simulator feedback to prioritize promising action sequences.
